title: Sex-Dependent Impact of Low-Level Lead Exposure during Prenatal Period on Child Psychomotor Functions.
ehr_evidence: The study uses the Polish Mother and Child Cohort (REPRO_PL), which involves questionnaire data and biological samples (blood), not electronic health records.
summary: Evaluation of the impact of low-level prenatal lead exposure on psychomotor development in children up to 24 months, specifically looking at sex-dependent susceptibility.
key_findings: No significant impact on girls' psychomotor abilities.
Boys showed lower cognitive scores associated with increasing cord blood lead levels (β = -2.07; p = 0.04).
Language and motor abilities were not significantly affected.
captured_features: Blood lead levels (20-24 weeks pregnancy, cord blood)
Bayley Scales of Infant and Toddler Development scores
population: 402 children from the Polish Mother and Child Cohort (REPRO_PL)
data_availability_statement: Associated Data Supplementary Materials Click here for additional data file.
